name: Clifford-Jacobs Employees
description_short: Clifford-Jacobs Employees Credit Union is a state-chartered credit union in Champaign, IL, established in 1956, serving over 200 members with savings accounts, auto loans, and personal banking.
description_long:
Clifford-Jacobs Employees Credit Union was established in 1956 and is headquartered in Champaign, Illinois. As a state-chartered credit union operating under Illinois law, it serves the local community as a not-for-profit financial institution. The credit union has grown to serve over 200 loyal members and maintains assets exceeding $1.2 million.

The institution offers a comprehensive range of financial products including regular savings accounts, share draft accounts, money market accounts, share certificates (CDs), auto loans for new and used vehicles, personal loans, mortgages, and online bill pay services. Members can access their accounts through online banking and mobile apps available on both iOS and Android platforms. The credit union provides competitive interest rates, with new vehicle loans at 4.99% APR and personal loans at 12.00% APR, reflecting rates consistent with not-for-profit lending models.

As a credit union, Clifford-Jacobs distinguishes itself through its not-for-profit structure, which allows it to offer lower fees and competitive rates compared to traditional for-profit banks. The institution maintains a single branch location at 2410 North 5th Street in Champaign and operates with over one dedicated employee as of 2026. Members benefit from financial calculators, a community Q&A forum, and personalized financial guidance.

Potential members should note that Clifford-Jacobs is a very small, employee-focused credit union with limited physical presence (1 branch, 0 ATMs) and a modest membership base of 200+ members. While it offers standard banking services and competitive rates, the limited branch network and ATM access may restrict convenience for members seeking broader physical accessibility or those relocating outside the Champaign area. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Not-for-profit structure offers lower fees and competitive rates compared to for-profit banks
New vehicle auto loans available at 4.99% APR, competitive rate for credit union lending
Mobile banking app available for both iOS and Android devices
Online bill pay service included for member convenience
Established in 1956 with over 65 years of community banking history
Financial calculators and educational resources available to members
Community Q&A forum provides peer advice and expert guidance
cons: Only 1 branch location with 0 ATMs, severely limiting physical access and convenience
Very small membership base of 200+ members may indicate limited resources or service capacity
Personal loans and used vehicle loans carry 12.00% APR, which is significantly higher than new vehicle rates
Currently no share draft (checking) or money market accounts with balances listed (0+)
Lobby currently closed at time of data collection, limiting in-person services
best_for: Clifford-Jacobs employees and their family members seeking membership eligibility
Champaign, Illinois residents wanting to support a local, not-for-profit credit union
Borrowers seeking new auto loans at competitive 4.99% rates without membership barriers
Members willing to conduct banking primarily online and via mobile app due to limited branches
diagnosis: Clifford-Jacobs Employees Credit Union is best suited for Champaign-area residents and eligible employees seeking a small, community-focused credit union with competitive rates on vehicle loans and not-for-profit banking advantages. The primary caveat is severe geographic limitation—with only one branch and zero ATMs, members must be comfortable with online banking and mobile app usage, and the institution is practical only for those based in or near Champaign, Illinois.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
